_comment: Venues that appear under more than one name across our sources. Both the
nightly fetch (scripts/fetch_events.py) and the browser (js/sources.js)
rewrite any variant to its canonical name BEFORE comparing venues, so the
same show listed by two sources collapses into one card.
WHY THIS EXISTS: duplicate detection requires the two venue names to share
tokens. A renamed venue defeats that -- 'Southwest Center Mall' and 'The
Shops at Redbird' are the same address with no word in common, so the same
UniverSoul Circus run showed up twice, three shows each.
WHY THIS IS THE SAFE FIX: an alias only ever causes a merge when the two
listings ALSO share a meaningful title word and start within 90 minutes of
each other. Loosening the venue comparison itself is what previously
collapsed Jackie Fabulous into Cipha Sounds -- don't do that; add data here
instead.
ONLY add a genuine rename or spelling variant of ONE physical place. Do NOT
add two rooms in the same building (House of Blues vs its Cambridge Room,
South Side Ballroom vs South Side Music Hall) -- those run different shows
on the same night and must stay separate. Same-name venues in different
cities (Hyena's Fort Worth vs Hyena's Dallas) are already kept apart by the
city gate and must not be aliased.
Matching ignores case, punctuation, '&' vs 'and', and a trailing ' - City'.
So 'Coopers Bar & Grill - Arlington' already matches 'Coopers Bar and Grill'
without an entry here.

The Shops at Redbird: Universoul Circus - Dallas - The Shops at Redbird
